case_title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS VS. STANLEY D BLACKWELL

docket_entries.description: APPLICATION FOR ENTRY OF SISTER STATE JUDGMENT; NOTICE OF SISTER STATE JUDGMENT ISSUED FILED BY PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS AS TO DEFENDANT BLACKWELL, STANLEY D JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION, PROOF OF SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 100029487) FILED BY PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS HEARING SET FOR OCT-03-2017 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, STANLEY D. BLACKWELL'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING. THE COURT DECIDES NOT TO MAKE A DETERMINATION ON THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION AT THIS TIME. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF THE VALIDITY OF THE JUDGMENT ENTERED ON APRIL 28, 2017. THE COURT ORDERS THE LEVYING OFFICER TO RELEASE ANY AND ALL FUNDS AND/OR BANK ACCOUNTS TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLENE P. KIESSELBACH; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG. (L&M302)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alisa J. Kim of the law offices of Alisa J. Kim (415-826-9160) appears as counsel for judgment creditor/opposing party

Stanley D. Blackwell, In Propria Persona (707-494-4390) appears by telephone as self-represented judgment debtor/claimant

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Stanley D. Blackwell's Claim of exemption is off calendar without prejudice to re-filing. The Court decides not to make a determination on the claim of exemption at this time. There is insufficient evidence of the validity of the judgment entered on April 28, 2017. The Court orders the levying officer to release any and all funds and/or bank accounts to the judgment debtor. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
